name: The Moonwell Clearing
description: The trees fall away around a pond so still it holds the daylight like a second sky. A worn path cuts through the grass toward the south, and the air smells faintly of crushed herbs.

entities.name: Shizuku
entities.description: An elf herbalist kneeling among cuttings, sleeves tied back, moving with unhurried care.
entities.nameMeaning: 雫 (shizuku, "droplet") — patient and quiet; she heals the way water wears stone, one drop at a time

art.subject: elf herbalist with tied-back sleeves kneeling among herb cuttings

lines.text: Careful where you step — the moonherb only flowers once a season.
You're the one the summons dragged through, aren't you. The pond went bright the night you arrived.

choices.label: "You know about the summoning?"
choices.reply: Everyone this side of the ridge knows. Her Grace does not summon gently — and she does not summon for nothing.

choices.label: Ask what the herbs are for.
choices.reply: Wounds, mostly. There are more of those than there used to be.

entities.name: Moonherb
entities.description: A pale flower growing at the pond's edge, petals faintly luminous.

lines.text: You ease the moonherb out of the soil. The glow does not fade.

interaction.afterText: Only a shallow depression in the soil remains.

transition.hint: The track leads toward the village Shizuku mentioned; wounded travelers have been coming from there.
